Victorian Legislation and Parliamentary Documents 
  
1   Version No. 011  
Educational Grants Act 1973 
Act No. 8495/1973 
Version incorporating amendments as at 1 July 1997 
 
An Act to make Provision with respect to Financial Aid for 
certain Schools. 
 
BE IT ENACTED by the Queen's Most Excellent Majesty by 
and with the advice and consen t of the Legislative Council 
and the Legislative Assembly of  Victoria in this present 
Parliament assembled and by th e authority of the same as 
follows (that is to say): 
 1. Short title and commencement 
 (1) This Act shall be cited as the  Educational Grants 
Act 1973 . 
 (2) This Act shall come into operation on the 1st day 
of January, 1974. 
 2. Definitions 
In this Act, unless inconsistent with the context or 
subject-matter— 
"non-government school"  means a school, other 
than a school conducted by the State at which full-time education is provided for pupils in primary or secondary classes, at which primary education or secondary education is, or both are, provided, but does not include a school conducted for the profit, direct or indirect, of an individual or individuals; 
  
Victorian Legislation and Parliamentary Documents 
 Act No. 8495/1973  
   
Educational Grants Act 1973 
2   "primary pupil"  means a child who is included 
on the school roll of a non-government school on the schools census date and who is receiving full-time education provided for pupils at that school being education of a kind similar to that provided for pupils in primary classes in sc hools conducted by the 
State; 
"qualified accountant"  means— 
 (a) a person who is registered as a 
company auditor; 
 (b) a member of the Institute of Chartered 
Accountants in Australia or of the Australian Society of Accountants; or 
 (c) a person approved by the Minister as a 
person competent to furnish such statements; 
"recurrent expenditure" , in relation to a non-
government school, means expenditure incurred for purposes related to the provision of primary education or  secondary education 
at the school; 
"school authority" , in relation to a non-
government school, means the person or body conducting the school; 
"secondary pupil"  means a child who is included 
on the school roll of a non-government school on the schools census date and who is receiving full-time education provided for pupils of that school being education of a kind similar to that provided for pupils in secondary classes in schools conducted by 
the State; 
  s. 2 
Victorian Legislation and Parliamentary Documents 
 Act No. 8495/1973  
   
Educational Grants Act 1973 
3   "schools census date"  means the date in the year 
as at which the Commonwealth Statistician compiles statistics in relation to the numbers of pupils in schools in this State; 
"year"  means a calendar year ending on the 31st 
day of December, 1983. 
    
 3. Capitation grants 
 (1) Subject to this section the Minister may pay 
capitation grants to the school authority of a non-government school. 
  (2) Any payment made under sub-section (1) shall be 
subject to the following conditions: 
 (a) The school authority will ensure that 
amounts equal to the total of all payments made by the Minister to the school authority in respect of the school in accordance with this section are applied in that year or not later than three months after the end of that year for the purposes of recurrent expenditure in respect of the school; 
 (b) The school authority will cause to be 
furnished to the Minister not later than six months after the end of the year in which the payment is made a statement in writing signed by a qualified accountant to the effect that he has satisfied himself that the condition referred to in the last preceding sub-paragraph has been  complied with and S. 2 def. of 
"year" amended by 
Nos 8632 s. 2, 
8790 s. 2, 8930 s. 2, 9062 s. 2, 
9220 s. 2, 9352 
s. 2, 9497 s. 2, 
9661 s. 2, 9830 
s. 2. s. 3 
S. 3(1) amended by 
No. 31/1994 
s. 3(Sch. 1 item 22). 
Victorian Legislation and Parliamentary Documents 
 Act No. 8495/1973  
   
Educational Grants Act 1973 
4   specifying the amount or amounts paid to the 
school authority in respect of that year; 
 (c) The school authority will cause to be 
furnished to the Minister, not later than six months after the end of the year in which the payment is made a statement in writing specifying the total income derived and the total expenditure incurred by the school authority in respect of primary and secondary education at that school in respect of that year and also specifying in respect of each class of income or class of expenditure determined by the Minister the total amount of income of that cla ss derived or the total 
amount of expenditure of that class incurred by the school authority in respect of primary and secondary education at that school in respect of that year; 
 (d) If the total of the grants paid under this 
section to a school authority in respect of the year exceeds the amount payable in accordance with the provisions of this section, the school authority will repay to the Minister the amount of the excess. 
 (3) Where the Minister is  satisfied that the school 
authority has failed to fulfil a condition to which the payment is made subject by this Act the Minister may direct that an amount equal to the whole or a part of a payment made to the school authority under this section shall be deducted from further amounts becoming payable to the school authority under this Act or under any corresponding subsequent enactment. 
 (4) Payments to a school authority under this section 
may be made at such times and in such 
instalments as the Minister determines. s. 3 
Victorian Legislation and Parliamentary Documents 
 Act No. 8495/1973  
   
Educational Grants Act 1973 
5    (5) In this section a capitation grant means a grant 
of— 
 (a) $374 in respect of each primary pupil;     
 
  (b) $611 in respect of each secondary pupil.       
 4. Regulations 
The Governor in Council may make regulations 
for or with respect to prescribing any matter or thing that is authorized or required to be prescribed and that is convenient to be prescribed to enable effect to be given to this Act. 
 5. Repeal 
 (1) The Educational Grants Act 1972  is hereby 
repealed . 
 (2) The Educational Grants Act 1972  
notwithstanding its repeal shall continue to have 
the same operation and effect  in and in relation to 
the year 1973 as it would have had if it had not 
been  repealed. S. 3(5)(a)

6    6. Saving in respect of previous year 
  The provisions of this Act as in force immediately 
prior to the commencement of the Educational 
Grants (Continuation) Act 1974  shall continue 
to have the same operatio n and effect in and in 
relation to the year 1974 as  it would have had if 
the Educational Grants (Continuation) Act 
1974  had not been passed. 
 7. Saving in respect of previous year 
  The provisions of this Act as in force immediately 
prior to the commencement of the Educational 
Grants (Continuation) Act 1975  shall continue 
to have the same operatio n and effect in and in 
relation to the year 1975 as they would have had if the Educational Grants (Continuation) Act 
1975  had not been passed. 
 8. Saving in respect of previous year 
  The provisions of this Act in force immediately 
prior to the commencement of section 3 of the 
Educational Grants (Continuation) Act 1976  
shall continue to have  the same operation and 
effect in and in relation to the year 1976 as they would have had if that  section had not been 
passed. 
 9. Saving in respect of previous year 
  The provisions of this Act in force immediately 
prior to the commencement of section 3 of the 
Educational Grants (Continuation) Act 1977  
shall continue to have  the same operation and 
effect in and in relation to the year 1977 as they would have had if that  section had not been 
passed.

1. General Information 
The Educational Grants Act 1973  was assented to on 4 December 1973 and 
came into operation on 1 January 1974: section 1(2) Endnotes
